persona_json.name: Marcus, the Production-First Infrastructure Engineer
persona_json.summary: Marcus is a senior infrastructure engineer at a mid-sized SaaS or fintech company who demands production-grade tooling. He's deeply invested in CI/CD automation, infrastructure-as-code, and API reliability, but he's skeptical of GraphQL implementations that haven't proven themselves under real-world load and deeply concerned about scale—especially when exporting hundreds of thousands of records.

location_signals: North America (implied by SaaS/fintech tech hubs)
demographics.education_level: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or equivalent

goals: Establish reliable CI/CD pipelines that integrate seamlessly with GitHub and reduce deployment friction
Implement infrastructure-as-code practices using Terraform to achieve repeatable, auditable deployments
Build robust API-driven data export capabilities that scale to hundreds of thousands of records
pains: Timeout failures and failed exports when handling large datasets (500k+ rows)
Lack of async export options that allow long-running data operations to complete reliably
Managing custom dashboard logic without native platform support
Inconsistency and instability in GraphQL implementations that break under production load
Limited visibility into system performance during large data operations
motivations: Ship code confidently with CI/CD automation and GitHub integration that reduces manual steps
Reduce operational toil through infrastructure-as-code and automation
Ensure platform reliability under production load
Enable other engineers with well-designed tooling and clear API contracts
objections: Won't adopt a tool if it can't handle production scale requirements
Skeptical of GraphQL implementations that haven't proven stability
Resistance to vendor lock-in; prefers standard, interoperable technologies
Concerned about timeout behavior and lack of async export options
decision_triggers: Proof that bulk export can handle 500k+ rows without timing out
Clear async/queuing mechanisms for long-running operations
Documented production case studies from similar-scale companies
REST API stability and performance benchmarks
Zero-vendor-lock-in guarantees and standard tech stack
Transparent performance monitoring and SLA commitments
sample_quotes: Your REST API is solid but the GraphQL endpoint has some rough edges.
We need the bulk export to handle 500k rows without timing out.
I need to know this will work under production load before we commit to it.
Give me infrastructure-as-code templates and clear API contracts, and I can scale this myself.

journey_stages.mindset: Evaluating whether the platform meets non-negotiable production requirements
content_preferences: case studies from production users at scale

journey_stages.mindset: Assessing integration depth with GitHub, Terraform, and CI/CD infrastructure
key_actions: Test bulk export with realistic data volumes

journey_stages.mindset: Verifying reliability, scale, and lack of vendor lock-in before full adoption
